description: CLI interface for Podio API - Manage apps, items, tasks, and more

get.help: Get a Podio item by ID or external ID.
get.usage_instructions: Use to retrieve a single item by ID. Optional --external-id/-e with --app-id/-a for external ID lookup. Use --basic for minimal info. Use --table/-t for formatted output.

list.help: List items in a Podio application with optional filtering.

arguments.name: app_id
arguments.help: Application ID to list items from

options.name: --filter
options.help: Filter: field:op:value (e.g., 'status:eq:active', 'title:contains:report'). Ops: eq,ne,gt,gte,lt,lte,in,nin,like,ilike,null,notnull,contains,startswith,endswith. Also supports JSON filter for Podio API fields

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of items to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include in output

list.usage_instructions: Use to list items in an app. APP_ID is required. Use --filter/-f for field-based filtering (supports eq, ne, gt, contains, etc. and JSON filter syntax). Use --sort-by and --desc for ordering. Default limit is 100.

create.help: Create a new item in a Podio application. Reads item data from a JSON file or stdin. Podio notifications are suppressed by default. Item data format: { "fields": [ {"external_id": "title", "values": [{"value": "Item Title"}]}, {"external_id": "status", "values": [{"value": "active"}]} ] }

arguments.name: app_id
arguments.help: Application ID to create item in

options.name: --json-file
options.help: Path to JSON file with item data

create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new item. APP_ID is required. Provide field data via --json-file (JSON with fields array). Notifications are suppressed by default (--silent). Use --no-hook to skip webhooks.

update.help: Update an existing Podio item. Reads update data from a JSON file or stdin. Update data format (same as create): { "fields": [ {"external_id": "status", "values": [{"value": "completed"}]} ] }

options.name: --json-file
options.help: Path to JSON file with update data

update.usage_instructions: Use to update an existing item. ITEM_ID is required. Provide update data via --json-file. Use --silent to suppress notifications. Use --no-hook to skip webhooks.

delete.usage_instructions: Use to delete an item. ITEM_ID is required. Use --silent to suppress notifications. Use --no-hook to skip webhooks.

values.help: Get field values for a specific item. Returns all field values in a clean format using the v2 API endpoint.

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.help: Item ID to get values from

values.usage_instructions: Use to get all field values for an item in clean format (v2 API). ITEM_ID is required.

field-value.help: Get a specific field's values for an item (v2 endpoint). The field can be specified either as: - field_id (e.g., 274720804) - external_id (e.g., "potential-writer")

arguments.name: item_id
arguments.help: Item ID to get field value from

arguments.name: field
arguments.help: Field ID or external_id to retrieve

field-value.usage_instructions: Use to get a specific field's values. ITEM_ID and FIELD (field_id or external_id) are required positional args.

item.usage_instructions: Item CRUD operations -- get, list, create, update, delete items in Podio apps. Use `item list APP_ID` with --filter for querying. Use `item values ITEM_ID` to see field values and `item field-value ITEM_ID FIELD` for a specific field.

get.help: Get a Podio application by ID. By default, deleted fields are excluded from the response. Use --include-deleted to show all fields including deleted ones. Use --fields to return only the field schema.

options.name: --include-deleted
options.help: Include deleted fields in the response

get.usage_instructions: Use to get app details. APP_ID is required. Use --fields/-f to return only field schema. Use --include-deleted to show deleted fields.

list.help: List all applications in a space. If space_id is not provided, uses PODIO_WORKSPACE_ID from environment. Filter examples: --filter "status:active" --filter "name:contains:Sales"

options.name: --space-id
options.help: Space ID to list apps from (defaults to PODIO_WORKSPACE_ID)

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list apps in a space. Uses PODIO_WORKSPACE_ID env var if --space-id/-s not provided. Supports --filter/-f for status/name filtering.

items.help: Get all items from an application.

arguments.name: app_id
arguments.help: Application ID to get items from

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of items to return

items.usage_instructions: Use to get all items from an app. APP_ID is required. Default limit is 30. Supports pagination with --offset.

activate.usage_instructions: Use to activate a deactivated app. APP_ID is required.

deactivate.usage_instructions: Use to deactivate an app. APP_ID is required.

create.help: Create a new Podio application. The app configuration must include space_id, config, and fields. You can either provide a JSON file with --json-file or pipe JSON via stdin.

options.name: --space-id
options.help: Space ID to create app in (defaults to PODIO_WORKSPACE_ID)

create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new app. Provide config via --json-file (must include space_id, config, fields).

update.help: Update a Podio application's configuration. You can update the app configuration using a JSON file or by specifying individual options. When using options, only the specified values are updated. Config properties that can be updated: - name: App name - item_name: Item name (singular) - description: App description (supports markdown) - icon_id: Icon ID (1-650) - allow_edit, allow_attachments, allow_comments - disable_notifications, silent_creates, silent_edits - And more (use --json-file for full control)

options.name: --json-file
options.help: JSON file with app configuration to update

options.name: --description
options.help: App description (supports markdown, shows in sidebar)

update.usage_instructions: Use to update app configuration. APP_ID is required. Provide updates via --json-file.

export.help: Export a Podio application to Excel. This command exports all items from an app to an Excel file. The export is processed asynchronously by Podio, so this command will poll for completion and download the file when ready.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of items to export

export.usage_instructions: Use to export app items as XLS or CSV. APP_ID is required. Use --format for output format.

add.help: Add a new field to an application. Supported field types: text, number, image, date, app, money, progress, location, duration, contact, calculation, embed, question, file, tel You can either provide --type and --label options, or use --json-file for full field configuration. When using --json-file, --type and --label are not required.

arguments.name: app_id
arguments.help: Application ID to add field to

options.name: --type
options.help: Field type (text, number, image, date, app, money, progress, location, duration, contact, calculation, embed, question, file, tel)

options.name: --json-file
options.help: JSON file with full field configuration (overrides other options)

options.name: --mimetypes
options.help: Allowed mimetypes for file fields (comma-separated, e.g., 'application/*,image/*')

add.usage_instructions: Add a new field to an application. Supported field types: text, number, image, date, app, money, progress, location, duration, contact, calculation, embed, question, file, tel You can either provide --type and --label options, or use --json-file for full field configuration. When using --json-file, --type and --label are not required. Required arguments: app_id. Notable options: --type, --label/-l, --required/-r, --json-file/-f, --mimetypes/-m, --table/-t. Example: `podio app field add 12345 --type text --label "Title"`.

get.help: Get a field from an application.
get.usage_instructions: Get a field from an application. Required arguments: app_id, field_id. Notable options: --table/-t. Example: `podio app field get 12345 67890`.

update.help: Update a field in an application. Only attributes explicitly provided in the JSON file are modified. All other field properties (delta, required, hidden, settings, etc.) are automatically preserved from the current field configuration.
update.usage_instructions: Update a field in an application. Only attributes explicitly provided in the JSON file are modified. All other field properties (delta, required, hidden, settings, etc.) are automatically preserved from the current field configuration. Required arguments: app_id, field_id. Notable options: --json-file/-f, --table/-t. Example: `podio app field update 12345 67890 --json-file field.json`.

delete.help: Delete a field from an application. WARNING: This will delete all data stored in this field for all items. When --delete-values is specified, field values are cleared from all existing items before the field is deleted. This works around a Podio API limitation where delete_values=true does not actually remove data from items.

options.name: --delete-values
options.help: Also delete field values from existing items

delete.usage_instructions: Delete a field from an application. WARNING: This will delete all data stored in this field for all items. When --delete-values is specified, field values are cleared from all existing items before the field is deleted. This works around a Podio API limitation where delete_values=true does not actually remove data from items. Required arguments: app_id, field_id. Notable options: --force/-f, --delete-values/-d, --table/-t. Example: `podio app field delete 12345 67890`.

list.help: List all fields in an application. By default, only active fields are shown. Use --include-deleted to show all fields.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List all fields in an application. By default, only active fields are shown. Use --include-deleted to show all fields. Required arguments: app_id. Notable options: --include-deleted/-d, --limit/-l, --filter/-f, --properties/-p, --table/-t. Example: `podio app field list 12345`.

field.usage_instructions: Use to get details of a specific field in an app. APP_ID and FIELD_ID are required.

app.usage_instructions: Application management -- get app details and field schemas, list apps in a space, create/update apps, get app items, activate/deactivate, and export app data. Use `app get APP_ID --fields` to see field schema.

list.help: List tasks for the authenticated user. Supports filtering, grouping, and sorting via API parameters. Filter examples: --filter "status:active" --filter "text:contains:urgent"

options.name: --grouping
options.help: Group by: due_date, created_by, responsible, app, space, org

list.usage_instructions: Use to list tasks. Supports --responsible/-r, --status/-s (active/completed), --label/-l, --reference for filtering.

get.help: Get a Podio task by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get task details. TASK_ID is required.

create.help: Create a new Podio task. Task data can be provided via JSON file/stdin or command-line options. Task data format: { "text": "Task description", "description": "Detailed description", "due_date": "2025-01-15", "private": false, "ref_type": "item", "ref_id": 12345 }

options.name: --json-file
options.help: Path to JSON file with task data

create.usage_instructions: Use to create a task. TEXT is required. Use --responsible/-r for assignee, --due-date/-d for deadline, --description for details.

complete.help: Mark a Podio task as complete.
complete.usage_instructions: Use to mark a task as completed. TASK_ID is required.

delete.usage_instructions: Use to delete a task. TASK_ID is required.

options.name: --json-file
options.help: Path to JSON file with update data

update.usage_instructions: Use to update a task. TASK_ID is required. Update fields with options like --text, --due-date, --responsible, --description.

list.help: List all task labels for the authenticated user.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List all task labels for the authenticated user. Notable options: --limit/-l, --filter/-f, --properties/-p, --table/-t. Example: `podio task label list`.

get.help: Get a specific task label by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Get a specific task label by ID. Required arguments: label_id. Notable options: --table/-t. Example: `podio task label get 12345`.

options.name: --color
options.help: Label color (hex code or color name)

create.usage_instructions: Create a new task label. Required arguments: text. Notable options: --color/-c, --table/-t. Example: `podio task label create "High Priority"`.

update.help: Update labels on a task. This replaces all existing labels.

arguments.name: task_id
arguments.help: Task ID to update labels on

update.usage_instructions: Update labels on a task. This replaces all existing labels. Required arguments: task_id. Notable options: --labels/-l, --table/-t. Example: `podio task label update 12345 --labels "123,456"`.

delete.usage_instructions: Delete a task label. Required arguments: label_id. Notable options: --table/-t. Example: `podio task label delete 12345`.

label.usage_instructions: Use to manage task labels. Subcommands for listing, creating, updating, deleting labels.

task.usage_instructions: Task management -- list, create, complete, update, delete, and label tasks. Use `task list` for all tasks, `task complete TASK_ID` to mark done.

get.help: Get a Podio space by ID or URL. If neither space_id nor url is provided, uses PODIO_WORKSPACE_ID from environment.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get space details. SPACE_ID is required.

list.help: List all spaces in an organization. If org_id is not provided, uses PODIO_ORGANIZATION_ID from environment.

options.name: --org-id
options.help: Organization ID to list spaces from (defaults to PODIO_ORGANIZATION_ID)

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list spaces in an org. ORG_ID is required.

space.usage_instructions: Space (workspace) management -- get space details and list spaces in an organization.

list.help: List all organizations the user is a member of. Returns organizations with their org_id and associated spaces.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list all organizations the user belongs to.

get.help: Get a Podio organization by ID. Returns organization details including name, spaces, and member info.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get organization details. ORG_ID is required.

org.usage_instructions: Organization management -- list organizations and get org details.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Prompts for required credentials based on the tool's authentication type. For OAuth authorization code flows, opens a browser for user consent.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

login.usage_instructions: Use to authenticate with Podio via OAuth. Opens browser for consent. Use --profile/-p for a named profile.

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

logout.usage_instructions: Use to clear stored credentials. Use --profile/-p for a specific profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to check (defaults to all profiles)

status.usage_instructions: Use to check current authentication status.

refresh.help: Refresh OAuth access token using stored refresh token.
refresh.usage_instructions: Use to refresh an expired OAuth token.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Use to verify credentials are valid and working.

list.help: List all profiles and show which is the default.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list all profiles and see which is the default.

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

get.usage_instructions: Use to get details of a specific profile. NAME is required.

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new profile. NAME is required.

set-default.help: Set a profile as the default (IS_DEFAULT_PROFILE=1).

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to set as default

set-default.usage_instructions: Use to switch the active profile. NAME is required.

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Use to delete a profile. NAME is required. Use --force/-F to skip confirmation.

profiles.usage_instructions: Profile management -- list, get, create, set default, and delete authentication profiles.

auth.usage_instructions: Authentication management -- login via OAuth, logout, check status, refresh tokens, test credentials.

create.help: Add a comment to a Podio object (item, status, etc.). You can provide comment text directly with --text, or use a JSON file for more complex comments with attachments or embeds. Comment data format: { "value": "Comment text", "external_id": "Optional external ID", "file_ids": [123, 456], "embed_id": 789, "embed_url": "https://example.com" }

options.name: --json-file
options.help: Path to JSON file with comment data

examples: podio comment create item 12345 --text "This is a comment"
create.usage_instructions: Use to add a comment to a Podio object. Requires object type, ID, and comment text.

list.help: Get all comments on a Podio object.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list comments on an object.

get.help: Get a specific comment by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get a specific comment by ID.

update.help: Update an existing comment. This should only be used to correct spelling and grammatical mistakes. Update data format: { "value": "Updated comment text", "external_id": "Optional external ID", "file_ids": [123, 456], "embed_id": 789, "embed_url": "https://example.com" }

options.name: --json-file
options.help: Path to JSON file with updated comment data

update.usage_instructions: Use to update a comment. COMMENT_ID is required.

delete.usage_instructions: Use to delete a comment. COMMENT_ID is required.

comment.usage_instructions: Comment operations -- create, list, get, update, and delete comments on Podio objects.

get.help: Get a Podio webhook by ID. Returns webhook details including URL, status, and event type. Note: The Podio API does not have a direct endpoint to get a single webhook. This command uses the hook info endpoint which returns basic hook information.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get webhook details. WEBHOOK_ID is required.

create.help: Create a new webhook for a Podio object. Common hookable types: app, space Common event types: item.create, item.update, item.delete IMPORTANT - AUTOMATIC VERIFICATION: Immediately after creation, Podio sends a hook.verify event to your URL with a verification code. Your endpoint must handle this event and call 'podio webhook validate' with the code to activate the webhook. Until validated, the webhook remains 'inactive' and will not receive events. See 'podio webhook verify --help' for detailed verification information.

arguments.name: hookable_id
arguments.help: ID of the object to hook

options.name: --url
options.help: Webhook URL to receive POST requests

examples: podio webhook create app 12345 --url https://example.com/webhook --type item.update
podio webhook create app 12345 -u https://webhook.site/abc123 --type item.create
create.usage_instructions: Use to create a webhook for an object. Requires ref_type, ref_id, url, and event type.

list.help: List all webhooks for a Podio object. When listing for an app, this includes both app-level webhooks and field-level webhooks for all fields in the app.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list webhooks on an object.

verify.help: Request verification for a webhook. WEBHOOK VERIFICATION EXPLAINED: Webhook verification is a security handshake that proves you control the URL you're registering. When you create a webhook, Podio automatically sends a hook.verify event to your endpoint. Your endpoint must respond by calling the validate command with the code Podio provides. HOW VERIFICATION WORKS: 1. Create webhook - Podio creates webhook with 'inactive' status 2. Podio sends hook.verify event to your URL with a verification code 3. Your endpoint receives: {"type": "hook.verify", "hook_id": 123, "code": "abc123"} 4. You call: podio webhook validate 123 abc123 5. Webhook status changes from 'inactive' to 'active' This command manually requests verification if you need to re-verify a webhook. Normally, verification happens automatically when you create a webhook.

validate.help: Validate a webhook with the verification code. WEBHOOK VERIFICATION PROCESS: After creating a webhook, Podio sends a hook.verify event to your endpoint URL. This event contains a verification code that you must submit back to Podio to activate the webhook. Until validation completes, the webhook remains 'inactive' and will not receive events. VERIFICATION WORKFLOW: 1. Podio sends to your endpoint: {"type": "hook.verify", "hook_id": 123, "code": "abc123"} 2. Your endpoint must extract the hook_id and code 3. You run this command: podio webhook validate 123 abc123 4. Webhook activates and starts receiving real events WHY VERIFICATION IS REQUIRED: Verification proves you control the webhook URL. Without it, anyone could register webhooks pointing to URLs they don't own, which could spam third-party services or leak data to malicious endpoints. YOUR ENDPOINT REQUIREMENTS: - Must be publicly accessible (Podio needs to reach it) - Must handle hook.verify event type - Must respond within Podio's timeout period - Should implement logging to capture the verification code
validate.usage_instructions: Use to validate a webhook's URL.

update.help: Update a webhook URL. Note: This is implemented as delete + recreate since the Podio API does not support direct webhook updates. The webhook will get a new hook_id but maintain the same hookable_type, hookable_id, and event type.
update.usage_instructions: Use to update a webhook. WEBHOOK_ID is required.

delete.usage_instructions: Use to delete a webhook. WEBHOOK_ID is required.

get.help: Get a specific field-level webhook by ID.
get.usage_instructions: Get a specific field-level webhook by ID. Required arguments: hook_id. Notable options: --table/-t. Example: `podio webhook field get 12345`.

create.help: Create a field-level webhook that only fires when a specific field is updated.

arguments.name: field_id
arguments.help: Field ID to create webhook for

options.name: --url
options.help: Webhook URL to receive POST requests

examples: podio webhook field create 12345 -u https://webhook.site/abc123 --type item.update
create.usage_instructions: Create a field-level webhook that only fires when a specific field is updated. Required arguments: field_id. Notable options: --url/-u, --type, --table/-t. Example: `podio webhook field create 12345 --url https://example.com/webhook`.

list.help: List all webhooks for a specific field.

arguments.name: field_id
arguments.help: Field ID to list webhooks for

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List all webhooks for a specific field. Required arguments: field_id. Notable options: --limit/-l, --filter/-f, --properties/-p, --table/-t. Example: `podio webhook field list 12345`.

update.usage_instructions: Update a field-level webhook URL. Required arguments: hook_id. Notable options: --url/-u, --table/-t. Example: `podio webhook field update 98765 --url https://newurl.com/webhook`.

field.usage_instructions: Use to get webhook field details.

webhook.usage_instructions: Webhook management -- create, list, get, verify, validate, update, delete webhooks, and inspect webhook fields.

list.help: Get all conversations for the authenticated user. Returns conversations ordered by time of the last event.

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list conversations. Supports pagination with --limit and --offset.

get.help: Get a specific conversation including participants and messages. Only participants in the conversation are allowed to view it.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get a conversation by ID.

create.help: Create a new conversation with a list of users or on an object. Once started, participants cannot be changed (yet). Use --ref-type and --ref-id to create a conversation on a specific object. Conversation data format: { "subject": "Conversation subject", "text": "Message body", "participants": [123456, 789012], "file_ids": [111, 222] }

options.name: --json-file
options.help: Path to JSON file with conversation data

create.usage_instructions: Use to create a new conversation. Requires subject, text, and participants.

reply.help: Reply to an existing conversation. Reply data format: { "text": "Reply message", "file_ids": [111, 222], "embed_id": 333, "embed_url": "https://example.com" }

options.name: --json-file
options.help: Path to JSON file with reply data

examples: podio conversation reply 12345 --text "Thanks for the message"
reply.usage_instructions: Use to reply to a conversation. CONVERSATION_ID and TEXT are required.

arguments.name: conversation_id
arguments.help: Conversation ID to mark as read

mark-read.usage_instructions: Use to mark a conversation as read.

arguments.name: conversation_id
arguments.help: Conversation ID to mark as unread

mark-unread.usage_instructions: Use to mark a conversation as unread.

search.usage_instructions: Use to search conversations by text.

on-object.help: Get all conversations on a specific object.
on-object.usage_instructions: Use to get conversations on a Podio object.

add.help: Add participants to an existing conversation.

options.name: --users
options.help: Comma-separated list of user IDs to add

add.usage_instructions: Add participants to an existing conversation. Required arguments: conversation_id. Notable options: --users/-u, --table/-t. Example: `podio conversation participant add 12345 --users "123456,789012"`.

conversation.usage_instructions: Conversation/messaging -- list, get, create conversations, reply, mark read/unread, star/unstar, leave, search, view events, get conversations on objects, and manage participants.

upload.help: Upload a file to Podio. Returns the file_id which can be used to attach the file to items, tasks, comments, etc.
upload.usage_instructions: Use to upload a file. FILE_PATH is required.

attach.help: Attach an uploaded file to an object. Valid reference types are: item, task, comment, status, space

arguments.name: ref_type
arguments.help: Reference type: item, task, comment, status, or space

attach.usage_instructions: Use to attach an uploaded file to a Podio object. FILE_ID, REF_TYPE, and REF_ID are required.

list.help: List files attached to an object. Valid reference types are: item, task, comment, status, space

arguments.name: ref_type
arguments.help: Reference type: item, task, comment, status, or space

arguments.name: ref_id
arguments.help: Reference ID to list files from

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list files. Supports filtering and pagination.

get.usage_instructions: Use to get file details. FILE_ID is required.

options.name: --output
options.help: Output file path (defaults to original filename)

download.usage_instructions: Use to download a file. FILE_ID is required.

copy.help: Copy a file to create a new file with a new file_id. Useful when you need to attach the same file to multiple objects.
copy.usage_instructions: Use to copy a file. FILE_ID is required.

file.usage_instructions: File operations -- upload, attach to objects, list, get details, download, and copy files.

list.help: List all webforms for an application. Returns all webforms (active and disabled) configured on the given app. Filter examples: --filter "status:active" --filter "name:contains:Contact"

arguments.name: app_id
arguments.help: Application ID to list webforms from

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: Use to list webforms for an app. APP_ID is required.

get.help: Get a specific webform by ID. Returns full details including settings, fields, domains, and attachments configuration.
get.usage_instructions: Use to get webform details. FORM_ID is required.

submit.help: Submit data to a Podio webform via HTTP POST. This submits directly to the public webform URL, exactly like a browser would. No authentication required - uses the public webform endpoint. Field data format (using field external_id as keys): { "title": "My Title", "description": "Some text", "category": "Option 1" } File attachments require the webform to have attachments enabled. Use 'podio webform attachments status <form>' to check.

options.name: --json-file
options.help: Path to JSON file with field data

options.name: --attach
options.help: File(s) to attach to the submission (can be used multiple times)

examples: podio webform submit https://podio.com/webforms/30560419/2584779 -f data.json -a file1.pdf -a file2.docx
submit.usage_instructions: Use to submit data to a webform. FORM_ID is required with data via --json-file.

get.help: Get details about a specific field on a webform. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

arguments.name: form_id_or_url
arguments.help: Webform ID or URL (e.g., 2584779 or https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

arguments.name: field_id
arguments.help: Field ID to retrieve details for

get.usage_instructions: Get details about a specific field on a webform. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779). Required arguments: form_id_or_url, field_id. Notable options: --table/-t. Example: `podio webform field get 2584779 275324144`.

add.help: Add a field to a webform. Adds an app field to the webform so it appears on the public form. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779) Note: In the URL format /webforms/{app_id}/{form_id}, the form_id is the SECOND number.

arguments.name: form_id_or_url
arguments.help: Webform ID or URL (e.g., 2584779 or https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

arguments.name: field_id
arguments.help: Field ID to add to the webform

add.usage_instructions: Add a field to a webform. Adds an app field to the webform so it appears on the public form. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779) Note: In the URL format /webforms/{app_id}/{form_id}, the form_id is the SECOND number. Required arguments: form_id_or_url, field_id. Notable options: --table/-t. Example: `podio webform field add 2584779 275324144`.

remove.help: Remove a field from a webform. Removes an app field from the webform so it no longer appears on the public form. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779) Note: In the URL format /webforms/{app_id}/{form_id}, the form_id is the SECOND number.

arguments.name: form_id_or_url
arguments.help: Webform ID or URL (e.g., 2584779 or https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

arguments.name: field_id
arguments.help: Field ID to remove from the webform

remove.usage_instructions: Remove a field from a webform. Removes an app field from the webform so it no longer appears on the public form. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779) Note: In the URL format /webforms/{app_id}/{form_id}, the form_id is the SECOND number. Required arguments: form_id_or_url, field_id. Notable options: --table/-t. Example: `podio webform field remove 2584779 275324144`.

list.help: List all fields on a webform. Shows the field IDs currently configured on the webform. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779) Note: In the URL format /webforms/{app_id}/{form_id}, the form_id is the SECOND number.

arguments.name: form_id_or_url
arguments.help: Webform ID or URL (e.g., 2584779 or https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

list.usage_instructions: List all fields on a webform. Shows the field IDs currently configured on the webform. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779) Note: In the URL format /webforms/{app_id}/{form_id}, the form_id is the SECOND number. Required arguments: form_id_or_url. Notable options: --limit/-l, --filter/-f, --properties/-p, --table/-t. Example: `podio webform field list 2584779`.

field.usage_instructions: Use to get field details for a webform.

enable.help: Enable file attachments on a webform. When enabled, users can attach files when submitting the webform. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

arguments.name: form_id_or_url
arguments.help: Webform ID or URL (e.g., 2584779 or https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

enable.usage_instructions: Enable file attachments on a webform. When enabled, users can attach files when submitting the webform. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779). Required arguments: form_id_or_url. Notable options: --table/-t. Example: `podio webform attachments enable 2584779`.

disable.help: Disable file attachments on a webform. When disabled, users cannot attach files when submitting the webform. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

arguments.name: form_id_or_url
arguments.help: Webform ID or URL (e.g., 2584779 or https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

disable.usage_instructions: Disable file attachments on a webform. When disabled, users cannot attach files when submitting the webform. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779). Required arguments: form_id_or_url. Notable options: --table/-t. Example: `podio webform attachments disable 2584779`.

status.help: Check if file attachments are enabled on a webform. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

arguments.name: form_id_or_url
arguments.help: Webform ID or URL (e.g., 2584779 or https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779)

status.usage_instructions: Check if file attachments are enabled on a webform. The FORM_ID_OR_URL can be either: - A numeric form ID (e.g., 2584779) - A full webform URL (e.g., https://podio.com/webforms/12345/2584779). Required arguments: form_id_or_url. Notable options: --table/-t. Example: `podio webform attachments status 2584779`.

attachments.usage_instructions: Use to list attachments for a webform.

webform.usage_instructions: Webform management -- list forms for an app, get form details, submit form data, inspect form fields, and list form attachments.

clear.help: Remove all cached responses. Deletes every file in the cache directory ({storage_dir}/cache/) and reports how many files and bytes were freed.
clear.usage_instructions: Run `podio cache clear --help` for exact arguments and options.

cache.usage_instructions: Run `podio cache --help` for exact arguments and options.

usage_instructions: Use for managing Podio workspaces -- items, apps, tasks, comments, files, webhooks, conversations, and webforms. Commands follow `podio <group> <action>` pattern. Most commands support --table/-t for table output. Item operations are the most common -- use `item list APP_ID` to query app items and `item get ITEM_ID` for details. Requires OAuth authentication via `podio auth login`.
